title_en: Prayer in Al-Muqni': The Middle Tier of Hanbali Salah Law
title_ar: الصلاة في المقنع: المستوى الوسيط في أحكام الصلاة الحنبلية
content_en:
The prayer chapter of Al-Muqni' demonstrates the work's strength as an intermediate reference: comprehensive enough to address all the major questions students encounter, detailed enough to give the reasoning behind the positions, and compact enough to be used efficiently.

On the times of prayer, Al-Muqni' presents the Hanbali positions with their evidential basis. Each of the five daily prayers has a time of preference (waqt al-ikhtiyar) and a time of permissibility (waqt al-jawaz), with the Hanbali school generally following the positions of Imam Ahmad as transmitted by his students. Ibn Qudamah addresses the beginning and end times for each prayer, the special rulings for clouds and overcast skies that make time-determination difficult, and the ruling on the person who wakes up late and must pray after the preferred time has passed.

The conditions of prayer receive thorough treatment. On the qiblah, Al-Muqni' addresses the ruling for those at a distance who can only estimate the direction (ijtihad), what happens if it later becomes clear that the ijtihad was wrong, and the ruling for those who follow a compass or other aid in determining direction. Ibn Qudamah's engagement with the practical dimension of qiblah determination reflects his awareness that his students would face these questions in daily life.

The Hanbali school's treatment of the opening takbir as a pillar (rukn) rather than merely an obligation — meaning that any word other than 'Allahu Akbar' is invalid for beginning the prayer, unlike some positions in the Hanafi school — is explained in Al-Muqni' with the evidential reasoning from the prophetic sunnah. This represents one of the characteristic Hanbali positions that students must learn early.

Congregational prayer in Al-Muqni' is covered with more detail than a primer would allow. Ibn Qudamah discusses the conditions for valid following behind an imam, the ruling when a follower performs an action before the imam (which generally invalidates the prayer if it is a pillar), the conditions for the validity of prayer behind an imam of a different school, and the rulings on leading mixed-gender congregations. These details are practically important and the intermediate level is the appropriate place to introduce them.

The chapter on funeral prayer is thorough, covering the obligation to pray over every Muslim who dies (including children above a certain age and adults), the four takbirs, the specific supplications after each, and the Hanbali positions on who may be prayed over and who may not. The question of prayer over a Muslim who was executed for a serious crime — and the Hanbali position that the ruler may decline to lead the prayer personally while the general obligation remains — illustrates the level of detail Al-Muqni' provides.
title_id: Salat dalam Al-Muqni': Tingkatan Menengah Hukum Salah Hanbali
content_id:
Bab salat dalam al-Muqni' menunjukkan kekuatan karya ini sebagai referensi tingkat menengah: cukup komprehensif untuk membahas semua pertanyaan utama yang dihadapi pelajar, cukup rinci untuk memberikan alasan di balik posisi-posisi, dan cukup ringkas untuk digunakan sebagai teks yang dibaca dari awal hingga akhir tanpa menjadi terlalu memberatkan.

Ibnu Qudamah mendekati hukum salat dengan cara yang sangat sistematis. Beliau mendefinisikan syarat-syarat sah salat secara jernih, menyebutkan fardhu-fardhunya, dan kemudian membahas sunnah-sunnahnya dan hal-hal yang membatalkan salat. Posisi Hanbali tentang beberapa isu kontroversial — seperti mengangkat tangan dalam berbagai posisi salat, bacaan basmalah, dan qunut nazilah — disajikan dengan cukup konteks metodologis untuk dipahami oleh pelajar tingkat menengah.

Ibnu Qudamah sangat teliti dalam membedakan antara hal-hal yang termasuk rukun (yang jika ditinggalkan membatalkan salat), hal-hal yang termasuk wajib (yang jika ditinggalkan secara sengaja membatalkan salat namun dapat ditambal dengan sujud sahwi jika lupa), dan hal-hal yang termasuk sunnah (yang meninggalkannya tidak membatalkan salat). Pemetaan tiga tingkatan ini sangat berguna bagi pelajar dan mencerminkan ketelitian hukum yang khas dari mazhab Hanbali.

Salat berjamaah, imamah, salat Jumat, salat musafir, dan salat-salat sunnah mendapat pembahasan yang terinci. Pembahasan tentang salat berjamaah khususnya mencerminkan penekanan mazhab Hanbali pada pentingnya berjamaah: Ibnu Qudamah menyajikan argumen-argumen untuk pandangan bahwa salat berjamaah adalah fardhu kifayah (bahkan ada yang mengatakan fardhu 'ain), dan kondisi-kondisi di mana seseorang dimaafkan jika tidak berjamaah.
